title: Ultimate Trivia Challenge
description: 20-question general knowledge trivia spanning science, history, pop culture, geography, and tech — how many can you get right?
viralHook: Only 4% of people score 20/20 — can you?

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'Aurum', meaning gold.

questions.question: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, marking the end of the Cold War era.

questions.question: Which streaming service produced 'Stranger Things'?
questions.explanation: Stranger Things has been a flagship Netflix original since 2016.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra, not Sydney, is Australia's capital — a common misconception.

questions.question: What does 'HTTP' stand for?
questions.explanation: HTTP is the foundation of data communication on the World Wide Web.

questions.question: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: Adults have 206 bones; babies are born with around 270, which fuse over time.

questions.question: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.explanation: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between approximately 1503 and 1519.

questions.question: Which artist released the album 'Midnights' in 2022?
questions.explanation: Taylor Swift's 'Midnights' broke multiple streaming records on release in October 2022.

questions.question: Which country has the most natural lakes?
questions.explanation: Canada contains over 60% of the world's freshwater lakes.

questions.question: Which company created the Python programming language?
options: No company — created by Guido van Rossum
questions.correctAnswer: No company — created by Guido van Rossum
questions.explanation: Python was created by Dutch programmer Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991.

questions.question: What planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars appears red due to iron oxide (rust) on its surface.

questions.question: What ancient wonder was located in Alexandria, Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Lighthouse of Alexandria (Pharos) guided sailors into port and stood for over 1,500 years.

questions.question: In 'Breaking Bad', what is Walter White's alter ego called?
questions.explanation: Walter White adopts 'Heisenberg' as his drug kingpin persona, named after physicist Werner Heisenberg.

questions.question: The Amazon River flows primarily through which country?
questions.explanation: About 60% of the Amazon basin lies within Brazil.

questions.question: What year was the first iPhone released?
questions.explanation: Steve Jobs unveiled the first iPhone on January 9, 2007.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in a vacuum (approx.)?
questions.explanation: Light travels at approximately 299,792 km/s in a vacuum.

questions.question: Which empire was ruled by Genghis Khan?
questions.explanation: Genghis Khan founded the Mongol Empire, the largest contiguous land empire in history.

questions.question: Which movie won the first-ever Best Picture Oscar in 1929?
questions.explanation: Wings (1927) won Best Picture at the 1st Academy Awards ceremony in 1929.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City covers just 0.44 km² (110 acres), making it the world's smallest country.

questions.question: What does 'AI' stand for in technology?
questions.explanation: Artificial Intelligence refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines.

scoringTiers.label: Solid Contender 🥈
scoringTiers.message: Good effort! A bit more studying and you'll crush it.

scoringTiers.label: Keep Practicing 📚
scoringTiers.message: Room to grow — try again and beat your score!
